questions.q: What does the 'I.K.' in I.K. Gujral's name stand for?
questions.explanation: I.K. Gujral's full name was Inder Kumar Gujral.

questions.q: Which constitutional office did I.K. Gujral hold from 1997 to 1998?
questions.explanation: I.K. Gujral served as the Prime Minister of India from 1997 to 1998.

questions.q: I.K. Gujral is associated with which foreign-policy doctrine?
questions.explanation: He is associated with the 'Gujral Doctrine' on relations with neighbouring countries.

questions.q: Before entering politics, I.K. Gujral built a career in which field?
questions.explanation: He was a career diplomat before and during his political life.

questions.q: I.K. Gujral became Prime Minister at the head of which coalition?
questions.explanation: He led the United Front coalition as Prime Minister, continuing the arrangement from his predecessor.

questions.q: Whom did I.K. Gujral succeed as Prime Minister in 1997?
questions.explanation: He succeeded H.D. Deve Gowda as Prime Minister in 1997.

questions.q: The Gujral Doctrine deals primarily with India's relations with which group of countries?
options: Members of the Security Council only
questions.explanation: The Gujral Doctrine set out principles for India's relations with its neighbouring countries.

questions.q: In which year did I.K. Gujral's tenure as Prime Minister end?
questions.explanation: His tenure as Prime Minister ended in 1998.

questions.q: Which ministerial portfolio relating to foreign affairs is closely associated with I.K. Gujral before his premiership?
questions.explanation: Gujral served as Minister of External Affairs, where the Gujral Doctrine took shape.

questions.q: I.K. Gujral's deck subtitle, 'The Diplomat Premier,' highlights his background in which area?
questions.explanation: The 'Diplomat Premier' subtitle reflects his career in diplomacy and foreign relations.

questions.q: I.K. Gujral was sworn in as Prime Minister in which month and year?
questions.explanation: He was sworn in as Prime Minister in April 1997.

questions.q: During his premiership, I.K. Gujral was a member of which House of Parliament?
questions.explanation: Gujral was a member of the Rajya Sabha during his term as Prime Minister.

questions.q: Who succeeded I.K. Gujral as Prime Minister of India in 1998?
questions.explanation: Atal Bihari Vajpayee became Prime Minister in 1998, succeeding I.K. Gujral.

questions.q: A core principle of the Gujral Doctrine was that India, as the larger country, would do what with smaller neighbours?
questions.explanation: A key tenet of the Gujral Doctrine was that India would extend goodwill and accommodation to smaller neighbours without insisting on reciprocity.

questions.q: I.K. Gujral's United Front government was supported from outside by which national party?
questions.explanation: The Indian National Congress provided outside support to the United Front government Gujral led.

questions.q: I.K. Gujral served as India's ambassador to which country during his diplomatic career?
questions.explanation: Gujral served as India's ambassador to the Soviet Union, an important posting in his diplomatic career.

questions.q: I.K. Gujral became Prime Minister during the term of which Lok Sabha?
questions.explanation: He became Prime Minister in 1997 during the term of the 11th Lok Sabha.

questions.q: Which President of India was in office when I.K. Gujral served as Prime Minister?
options: Shankar Dayal Sharma and later K.R. Narayanan
questions.explanation: Gujral's 1997-1998 term spanned the presidencies of Shankar Dayal Sharma and then K.R. Narayanan.

questions.q: I.K. Gujral's premiership is best understood as part of which broader period of Indian national politics?
questions.explanation: His tenure was part of the era of coalition governments at the Union level.

questions.q: Before his term as Prime Minister, in which decade did I.K. Gujral first hold a Union ministerial post?
questions.explanation: Gujral first held Union ministerial responsibilities in the 1960s, well before becoming Prime Minister.

questions.q: I.K. Gujral was the External Affairs Minister in the government immediately preceding his own premiership. That government was led by whom?
questions.explanation: Gujral served as External Affairs Minister in the Deve Gowda government before becoming Prime Minister himself.

questions.q: As Prime Minister, I.K. Gujral headed which constitutional body collectively responsible to the Lok Sabha?
questions.explanation: As Prime Minister he headed the Council of Ministers, which is collectively responsible to the Lok Sabha.

questions.q: In which month and year did I.K. Gujral take the oath of office as Prime Minister?
questions.explanation: Gujral was sworn in as Prime Minister in April 1997 after Deve Gowda's government lost majority support.

questions.q: I.K. Gujral first served as Minister of External Affairs in an earlier government during which years?
questions.explanation: Gujral served an earlier stint as External Affairs Minister in the National Front government around 1989-1990.

questions.q: The Gujral Doctrine is generally summarised as a set of how many guiding principles for relations with neighbours?
questions.explanation: The Gujral Doctrine is commonly described as comprising five principles governing India's ties with its neighbours.

questions.q: I.K. Gujral entered the Rajya Sabha representing which state for much of his later parliamentary career?
questions.explanation: Gujral was elected to the Rajya Sabha from Bihar during a significant part of his parliamentary career.

questions.q: Which constitutional article provides for the appointment of the Prime Minister and other ministers, the framework under which Gujral took office?
questions.explanation: Article 75 governs the appointment of the Prime Minister and ministers, under which Gujral was appointed in 1997.

questions.q: I.K. Gujral's tenure as Prime Minister ended after which event in late 1997?
options: A merger of all coalition partners
Withdrawal of outside support to his coalition
questions.explanation: His government fell after the withdrawal of outside support, leading to the end of his tenure and fresh elections.

questions.q: Counting the United Front prime ministers in sequence, where does I.K. Gujral fall?
options: The second United Front Prime Minister
The first United Front Prime Minister
The third United Front Prime Minister
There was only one United Front Prime Minister
questions.explanation: Gujral was the second United Front Prime Minister, following H.D. Deve Gowda.

questions.q: I.K. Gujral's premiership is positioned in the sequence of Prime Ministers between which two figures?
options: P.V. Narasimha Rao and H.D. Deve Gowda
H.D. Deve Gowda and Atal Bihari Vajpayee
Atal Bihari Vajpayee and Manmohan Singh
Chandra Shekhar and P.V. Narasimha Rao
questions.explanation: Gujral served between H.D. Deve Gowda, whom he succeeded, and Atal Bihari Vajpayee, who succeeded him.
